Search Operator Licence

Search by name, licence number, or category — no CAPTCHA needed

This portal is protected by rate limiting — max 15 searches per minute per session. No CAPTCHA is required for legitimate queries.

How We Protect This Portal

We replaced CAPTCHA with smarter, frictionless security measures.

Rate Limiting Max 15 queries/min per session
Honeypot Fields Silent bot detection, zero friction
Behavioural Analysis Flags suspicious patterns silently
HTTPS + HSTS All traffic encrypted end-to-end

Home › About BOCRA
Est. 1 April 2013

About BOCRA

The Botswana Communications Regulatory Authority was established under the Communications Regulatory Authority Act, 2012 to regulate the communications sector in Botswana.

Profile

The Botswana Communications Regulatory Authority (BOCRA) was established through the Communications Regulatory Authority Act, 2012 (CRA Act) on the 1st of April 2013 to regulate the communications sector in Botswana, comprising telecommunications, Internet and Information and Communications Technologies (ICTs), radio communications, broadcasting, postal services and related matters.

The CRA Act replaced the Broadcasting Act [Cap 72:04], the Telecommunications Act [Cap 72:03], and caused the amendment of the Postal Services Act to create a converged or an integrated regulatory authority for the communications industry.

Mission

To regulate the Communications sector for the promotion of competition, innovation, consumer protection and universal access.

Vision

A connected and Digitally Driven Society.

Values

Excellence

We will strive to be the world-class leader in the provision of regulatory services through committed teams and impeccable customer service.

Proactiveness

We will be forward looking in the delivery of our mandate, to keep up with evolving industry trends.

Integrity

In the execution of our mandate we will demonstrate openness, honesty and accountability in all our decisions.

People

We believe that our people are key to driving our success through their commitment and excellence. We shall therefore harness and develop individual skills and strengths to work as one.

Core Business

The BOCRA will take the lead in areas that drive the advancement of the communications sector with the ultimate goal to drive the development of and enable universal access to enhanced services, economic opportunities, employment creation and sustainable growth necessary for the achievement of a Digitally Connected Society. To achieve this goal, BOCRA will pursue six key success factors or strategic pillars as follows:

Competition
Universal Access & Service
Consumer Protection
Quality of Service
Effective Regulation
Institutional Excellence
2013
Year Established
4
Regulatory Domains
CRA
Act 2012
CE

A Word From The Chief Executive

Chief Executive Officer, BOCRA

"As Botswana continues its digital transformation journey, BOCRA remains committed to creating a regulatory environment that fosters investment, innovation and the delivery of quality communications services to all citizens."

Our mandate extends beyond licensing and compliance — we are guardians of the public interest in the digital age. From ensuring affordable broadband access to protecting consumers from unfair practices, BOCRA's work touches every Motswana with a mobile phone, a postal address, or an internet connection.

We invite all stakeholders — citizens, industry players, civil society and government — to engage with us as we work together to build a connected, inclusive and prosperous Botswana.

History of Communication Regulation

1966

Botswana gains independence. Posts & Telecommunications operated under government ministry.

1996

Botswana Telecommunications Authority (BTA) established as an independent regulator.

2000

Broadcasting sector regulation introduced; national FM and TV licences issued.

2008

Policy review begins — convergence of communications sectors identified as priority.

2012

Communications Regulatory Authority Act, 2012 enacted by Parliament.

2013

BOCRA formally established on 1 April 2013, replacing BTA.

2016

bw-CIRT launched to manage national cybersecurity incidents.

2020

BOCRA leads response to COVID-19 communications continuity across Botswana.

2024

New broadband and spectrum frameworks aligned to SADC harmonisation agreements.

Home › Complaints
Consumer Affairs

Complaints &
Consumer Rights

File a complaint, track your case, or learn about your rights as a communications consumer in Botswana.

Your Rights

As a consumer you have the right to quality service, fair billing, transparent pricing, and a fair complaints process.

How It Works

Submit your complaint → Receive a reference number → BOCRA contacts the operator → Resolution within 30 working days.

Eligibility

You must have first raised the issue directly with the operator and waited at least 14 days for resolution before filing with BOCRA.

File & Track

Home › Mandate
Regulatory Authority

BOCRA Mandate

The legislative and regulatory mandate of the Botswana Communications Regulatory Authority across all communications sectors.

Telecommunications

Licensing of fixed, mobile and satellite operators; spectrum management; quality of service monitoring; interconnection regulation.

Broadcasting

Licensing of radio and TV broadcasters; content standards; signal distribution; must-carry obligations.

Postal Services

Licensing of postal operators; universal service obligations; postal quality standards; consumer protection.

Internet & ICT

ISP licensing; .bw domain registry; cybersecurity (bw-CIRT); e-commerce regulation; data protection coordination.

Consumer Protection

Consumer rights enforcement; complaints resolution; consumer education; market monitoring.

Legislation & Compliance

Developing regulations; enforcing the CRA Act; advisory role to government on communications policy.

Legislative Framework

BOCRA derives its mandate from the Communications Regulatory Authority Act, 2012 (CRA Act), which repealed and replaced the Broadcasting Act [Cap 72:04] and the Telecommunications Act [Cap 72:03], and amended the Postal Services Act.

BOCRA

Established under the Communications Regulatory Authority Act, 2012. Regulating telecommunications, broadcasting, postal and internet services in the public interest of Botswana.
